meaning: adj.神圣化的,认可的,批准的
examples: This was a practice sanctified by tradition.
God sanctified the Sabbath at creation; he demarcated it as holy.
For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
Now we find that maybe the public wants it broken, likes it this way, the divisions sanctified by 100 million choices.
In light of all this, the one ongoing certainty is that the mortgage walkers naively sanctified by Ryan Chittum will never be the victims.
